records.ety: [F. couple, fr. L. copula a bond, band; co- + apere, aptum, to join. See Art, a., and cf. Copula.]

defs: That which joins or links two things together; a bond or tie; a coupler.
quotes: It is in some sort with friends as it is with dogs in couples; they should be of the same size and humor.
I'll go in couples with her.

defs: Two of the same kind connected or considered together; a pair; a brace.
quotes: Adding one to one we have the complex idea of a couple.
[Ziba] met him with a couple of asses saddled.

defs: A male and female associated together; esp., a man and woman who are married or betrothed.
quotes: Such were our couple, man and wife.
Fair couple linked in happy, nuptial league.

defs: One of the pairs of plates of two metals which compose a voltaic battery; -- called a voltaic couple or galvanic couple.
Two rotations, movements, etc., which are equal in amount but opposite in direction, and acting along parallel lines or around parallel axes.
notes: ☞ The effect of a couple of forces is to produce a rotation. A couple of rotations is equivalent to a motion of translation.

records.ety: [F. coupler, fr. L. copulare. See Couple, n., and cf. Copulate, Cobble, v.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Coupled (kp"'ld); p. pr. & vb. n. Coupling (kp"lng).]

defs: To link or tie, as one thing to another; to connect or fasten together; to join.
quotes: Huntsman, I charge thee, tender well my hounds, . . . And couple Clowder with the deep-mouthed brach.

defs: To join in wedlock; to marry.
quotes: A parson who couples all our beggars.

defs: To come together as male and female; to copulate.
